Title: H3K36 demethylase JMJ710 negatively regulates drought tolerance by suppressing MYB48-1 expression in rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Site-directed mutations of conserved Fe(II)-binding residues (H340A/E342A) in the JmjC domain abolish H3K36me2 demethylase activity in vitro and in vivo, confirming that these residues and the intact JmjC domain are essential for enzymatic function.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT, JMJ710 overexpression, and JMJ710 RNAi plants under drought stress to identify differentially expressed drought-responsive genes (no accession numbers provided).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of drought and PEG-induced osmotic stress tolerance in rice; overexpression causes drought hypersensitivity, whereas RNAi knockdown and CRISPR loss-of-function mutants exhibit enhanced drought and osmotic tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: JMJ710 acts as an H3K36me2-specific demethylase; under normal conditions it binds the chromatin of MYB48-1 and ASR4 and demethylates H3K36me2, maintaining their low expression and limiting activation of drought-responsive genes; drought stress downregulates JMJ710 and reduces its occupancy at these loci, leading to increased H3K36me2 and higher target gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Epigenetic regulation of drought-responsive gene expression via H3K36me2 demethylation at target genes such as MYB48-1 and ASR4.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Histone H3K36 methylation / chromatin modification pathway.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: JMJ710 --(H3K36me2 demethylation; transcriptional repression)--> MYB48-1; JMJ710 --(H3K36me2 demethylation; transcriptional repression)--> ASR4.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JmjC domain-containing histone demethylase of the JMJD6 group with an additional F-box domain.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nuclear localization demonstrated by JMJ710-GFP fusion in Nicotiana benthamiana epidermal cells, rice root tips, and rice protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Ubiquitously expressed in multiple tissues (leaf, root, coleoptile, spikelet, stem, pistil, stamen) with higher transcript levels in roots, stems, and stamens; transcription is slightly reduced under drought or PEG6000-induced osmotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil-drying drought stress and PEG6000-induced osmotic stress (20% PEG6000) applied to seedlings; dehydration conditions for water loss assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought and osmotic stress tolerance regulated by histone demethylation.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and osmotic stress).
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Loss-of-function jmj710 alleles (CRISPR-induced truncations lacking the JmjC domain) confer enhanced drought and osmotic stress tolerance compared with WT and overexpression lines.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought stress, survival rates: jmj710-6 mutant 81.48%, RNAi lines 67.13% (R15) and 67.59% (R27), WT 36.11%, OE8 10.19%, OE9 7.41%. In long-term drought, survival rates: jmj710-6 79.17%, WT 41.67%, jmj710/myb48-1 22.92%, myb48-1 9.02%. Drought-tolerant lines display lower water loss from detached leaves than WT or OE lines.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Loss-of-function jmj710 mutants and myb48-1 or jmj710/myb48-1 mutants show no obvious morphological changes compared with WT under normal growth conditions.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa cv. Nipponbare WT; JMJ710 overexpression lines (OE8, OE9); JMJ710 RNAi lines (R15, R27); CRISPR/Cas9 knockout mutants jmj710-2 and jmj710-6; transgenic plants expressing JMJ710-HA or JMJ710-GFP; rice protoplasts; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Bioinformatic domain analysis; construction of overexpression, RNAi, and CRISPR/Cas9 gene-editing vectors; generation of transgenic rice lines; drought and PEG6000 osmotic stress assays; survival rate and growth measurements; water loss assays; RT-qPCR for tissue and stress-responsive expression; subcellular localization via GFP fusion and confocal microscopy; in vitro histone demethylation assays using purified JMJ710-His; in vivo histone methylation analysis by Western blotting of histone-enriched fractions; site-directed mutagenesis of Fe(II)-binding residues; ChIP-qPCR using anti-HA and anti-H3K36me2 antibodies; RNA-seq to identify differentially expressed drought-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression, RNAi knockdown, and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in rice combined with drought and PEG6000 osmotic stress assays; in vitro and in vivo histone demethylation assays; ChIP-qPCR to demonstrate direct binding to and H3K36me2 demethylation at target gene loci; genetic interaction analysis with myb48-1 and jmj710/myb48-1 double mutants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a JMJD6-group JmjC domain-containing protein in rice by bioinformatic analysis and cloned as full-length CDS for functional studies.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: JMJ710 is a promising target for breeding or genome editing of drought- and osmotic-tolerant rice: its downregulation or knockout enhances stress tolerance without obvious growth penalties under normal conditions, and it acts as a central epigenetic repressor of key drought-responsive genes such as MYB48-1 and ASR4.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Previously, overexpression of MYB48-1 was reported to confer drought tolerance in rice by activating OsP5CS1 and OsP5CS2; this study adds that MYB48-1 is epistatic to JMJ710 in drought tolerance regulation.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT, JMJ710 overexpression, and JMJ710 RNAi plants under drought stress identified MYB48-1 as one of the top differentially expressed drought-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of drought and osmotic stress tolerance; CRISPR myb48-1 knockout plants are hypersensitive to drought and PEG-induced osmotic stress, and loss of MYB48-1 partially suppresses the drought-tolerant phenotype of jmj710 mutants, indicating that MYB48-1 acts genetically downstream of JMJ710.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MYB48-1 expression is directly repressed by JMJ710-mediated H3K36me2 demethylation at its gene body under normal conditions; drought reduces JMJ710 expression and occupancy at MYB48-1, causing increased H3K36me2 levels and strong transcriptional activation of MYB48-1.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in a JMJ710–MYB48-1 epigenetic module that controls activation of downstream drought-responsive genes under dehydration stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: JMJ710 --(H3K36me2 demethylation; repression)--> MYB48-1; drought stress downregulates JMJ710, leading to increased H3K36me2 at MYB48-1 and strong induction of MYB48-1 expression.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB transcription factor family (MYB TRANSCRIPTION FACTOR 48).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expression is low under normal conditions and specifically upregulated by drought stress; transcript levels are reduced in JMJ710 overexpression plants and elevated in JMJ710-RNAi and jmj710 mutants under drought stress, but similar among genotypes under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress and PEG6000-induced osmotic stress applied to seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Positive regulation of drought and osmotic stress tolerance downstream of the histone demethylase JMJ710.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and osmotic stress).
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Functional MYB48-1 allele with strong drought-induced expression contributes to drought tolerance; CRISPR loss-of-function myb48-1 allele causes hypersensitivity to drought and osmotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under long-term drought stress, survival rates: myb48-1 mutant 9.02%, jmj710/myb48-1 double mutant 22.92%, jmj710-6 mutant 79.17%, WT 41.67%. myb48-1 and jmj710/myb48-1 show greater water loss from detached leaves than drought-tolerant jmj710-6.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: myb48-1 and jmj710/myb48-1 mutants show no obvious morphological changes compared with WT and jmj710-6 under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa cv. Nipponbare WT; CRISPR/Cas9 myb48-1 mutant; jmj710-6 mutant; jmj710/myb48-1 double mutant.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated gene editing to generate myb48-1 and jmj710/myb48-1 mutants; PEG6000 osmotic stress assays; long-term soil-drying drought assays; survival rate and shoot/root length measurements; water loss assays; RT-qPCR to assess MYB48-1 expression; ChIP-qPCR to measure JMJ710-HA enrichment and H3K36me2 levels at the MYB48-1 locus.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of MYB48-1 and construction of jmj710/myb48-1 double mutants combined with drought and osmotic stress assays, water loss measurements, and ChIP-qPCR of JMJ710 binding and H3K36me2 status at MYB48-1 chromatin.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously identified as a drought-responsive MYB transcription factor; in this study, CRISPR/Cas9 was used to generate a myb48-1 knockout and a jmj710/myb48-1 double mutant for functional analyses.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MYB48-1 is a key positive regulator downstream of JMJ710; enhancing MYB48-1 activity or expression, or relieving its repression via manipulation of JMJ710, could improve drought tolerance in rice, as loss-of-function myb48-1 is highly drought-sensitive and partially suppresses the drought-tolerant phenotype of jmj710 mutants.

Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Identified as a top differentially expressed gene from RNA-seq of WT, JMJ710 overexpression, and JMJ710 RNAi plants under drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downstream drought-responsive gene whose transcription is negatively regulated by JMJ710 under drought stress; although its own loss- or gain-of-function phenotype is not tested here, ASR4 is directly targeted by JMJ710 and shows JMJ710-dependent drought-inducible expression.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Under normal conditions, JMJ710 binds ASR4 chromatin and demethylates H3K36me2, maintaining low ASR4 expression; under drought stress, JMJ710 enrichment at ASR4 decreases, H3K36me2 at ASR4 increases, and ASR4 transcription is upregulated.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Part of the JMJ710-centered epigenetic module controlling drought-responsive gene expression via modulation of H3K36me2 levels.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: JMJ710 --(H3K36me2 demethylation; repression)--> ASR4; drought stress reduces JMJ710 enrichment at the ASR4 locus, leading to increased H3K36me2 and elevated ASR4 expression.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Asr (abscisic acid-, stress-, and ripening-inducible) family protein.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: ASR4 is induced by drought stress; its expression is downregulated in JMJ710 overexpression lines and upregulated in JMJ710-RNAi and jmj710 mutants under drought, but similar among genotypes under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress applied to rice seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought-responsive gene epigenetically repressed by JMJ710 via H3K36me2 demethylation.
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Higher drought-induced ASR4 expression, achieved by reduced JMJ710 activity at its locus, is associated with activation of drought tolerance mechanisms.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought stress, ASR4 transcript levels are lower in JMJ710 overexpression plants and higher in JMJ710-RNAi and jmj710 mutants than in WT, while similar among genotypes under normal conditions (relative fold changes shown by RT-qPCR). Drought stress reduces JMJ710 enrichment at ASR4 chromatin and increases H3K36me2 levels at the ASR4 locus.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa cv. Nipponbare WT; JMJ710 overexpression lines; JMJ710 RNAi lines; jmj710-6 mutant plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA-seq to identify ASR4 as a JMJ710-regulated drought-responsive gene; RT-qPCR to measure ASR4 expression in different JMJ710 genotypes under normal and drought stress; ChIP-qPCR using JMJ710-HA to detect binding at ASR4 chromatin; ChIP-qPCR with anti-H3K36me2 to assess histone methylation status at ASR4.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combination of RNA-seq, RT-qPCR, JMJ710-HA ChIP-qPCR, and H3K36me2 ChIP-qPCR under normal and drought conditions to verify that ASR4 is a direct target of JMJ710 and that its transcription responds to JMJ710-dependent changes in H3K36me2.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from RNA-seq of WT, JMJ710 overexpression, and JMJ710 RNAi plants under drought stress as a reported drought-related gene whose expression is negatively correlated with JMJ710; selected for further validation as a JMJ710 target.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: ASR4 is a JMJ710-regulated drought-responsive gene and part of the transcriptional network activated when JMJ710 is downregulated by drought; it represents a potential downstream target to monitor or manipulate when engineering JMJ710-based improvements in drought tolerance.
